---
title: "icosphere_hierarchy"
generated: true
description: "Construct a sequence of icospheres from level 0 to `max_level`."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.sparse.mesh`](../nitrix-sparse-mesh.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:icosphere_hierarchy -->
```python
icosphere_hierarchy(max_level: int) -> IcosphereHierarchy
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:icosphere_hierarchy -->
Construct a sequence of icospheres from level 0 to `max_level`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:icosphere_hierarchy -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `max_level` | `int` | — | — | Inclusive upper bound on the subdivision level. The hierarchy contains `max_level + 1` meshes, `[ico_0, ico_1, ..., ico_max_level]`. `max_level = 0` returns the base icosahedron alone. |

**Returns** `IcosphereHierarchy`. An [`IcosphereHierarchy`](../nitrix-sparse-mesh/IcosphereHierarchy.qmd) with `meshes` and `parents` tables populated (`parents[0] is None`).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:icosphere_hierarchy -->
::: {.callout-note}
Subdivision is deterministic (the midpoint cache assigns indices
by a fixed traversal order over faces), so the cross-level
helpers below depend only on the stored parent tables.
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
